dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9264 IH: No Exceptions for Non-Citizens Voting Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Burlison (for himself, Mr. Ogles, and Mr. Moore of Alabama) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend title 18, United States Code, to strike all exceptions to the circumstances in which it is unlawful for aliens to vote, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the No Exceptions for Non-Citizens Voting Act.

subsection.text: Section 611 of title 18, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: in subsection (a), by striking , unless— and all that follows through one or more of such Federal offices; and

subsection.text: Section 212(a)(10)(D) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1182(a)(10)(D)) is amended to read as follows:

subparagraph.text: Any alien who has voted in violation of any Federal, State, or local constitutional provision, statute, ordinance, or regulation is inadmissible.

subsection.text: Section 237(a)(6) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1227(a)(6)) is amended to read as follows:

paragraph.text: Any alien who has voted in violation of any Federal, State, or local constitutional provision, statute, ordinance, or regulation is deportable.
